Paths/Frontend Development/HTML Basics

HTML Basics

Learn the building blocks of every web page — elements, attributes, and document structure.

HTML (HyperText Markup Language) is the standard language for creating web pages. It describes the structure of a web page using a series of elements.

Key Concepts

  • Elements & Tags: HTML uses tags like `

    `, `

    `, `
    ` to define content.

  • Attributes: Tags can have attributes like `class`, `id`, `href` that provide extra information.
  • Document Structure: Every HTML page has ``, ``, ``, and ``.
  • Semantic HTML: Use tags like `
    `, `
    `, `
    `, `
    ` for meaningful structure.

Example

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <title>My First Page</title>
</head>
<body>
  <header>
    <h1>Hello, World!</h1>
  </header>
  <main>
    <p>This is my first web page.</p>
  </main>
</body>
</html>
